headers.value: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://www.googletagmanager.com https://www.google-analytics.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self' https://www.google-analytics.com https://region1.google-analytics.com https://ipapi.co https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org https://wa.me https://www.googletagmanager.com https://googleads.g.doubleclick.net https://www.google.com; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'

headers._lot4_comment: === Lot 4 — PAA deletion marker (377 URLs) — DELETE THIS BLOCK AFTER FILES ARE REMOVED FROM DISK ===
=== Lot 4 — ES deletion marker (6 URLs) — DELETE THIS BLOCK AFTER FILES ARE REMOVED FROM DISK ===
=== Lot 4 — ML deletion marker (13 URLs) — DELETE THIS BLOCK AFTER FILES ARE REMOVED FROM DISK ===

_lot4_warning: Vercel does NOT support 410 status code in vercel.json redirects. This draft adds X-Robots-Tag: noindex headers for fast deindexation. The DEFINITIVE method to signal 'deleted' is to REMOVE the .html files from disk. If files are removed: Vercel returns 404 (acceptable to Google), noindex header is moot. If files are KEPT on disk: X-Robots-Tag: noindex prevents further crawling, but the URL still returns 200 (Google may keep indexing until next crawl). For TRUE 410, see middleware.draft.ts.<repo> (Edge Middleware).

_lot4_strategy.step_1: DELETE the .html files in /tmp/atlas-lots-2-4-5-6/lot4-lists/ from the repo (scalpel)
_lot4_strategy.step_2: MERGE this draft's noindex headers into vercel.json (defensive layer)
_lot4_strategy.step_3: OPTIONAL: deploy middleware.draft.ts.<repo> for true 410 responses
